notes: The skill's biggest advantage is in structural consistency: with_skill outputs always follow the exact Happy path / Exceptions / Mermaid format, while without_skill outputs use varied formats (tables, ASCII art, different section names).

The vague-input test case shows a weakness: both with_skill and without_skill generate a full journey map instead of just asking for clarification. The skill adds a note with clarifying questions but still generates content — the Error Handling instruction to 'ask the user' is not being followed strictly.

Non-discriminating assertions: 'asks-about-product' and 'asks-about-users' pass in both configurations — both approaches mention the need for more information, they just differ in whether they stop or proceed.

The wishlist eval shows the without_skill baseline is surprisingly strong (7/8), suggesting Claude already has good journey-mapping capabilities. The skill primarily adds format discipline (phase count constraint) and prevents scope creep.
Token usage is similar across configurations — the skill does not significantly increase cost.
